{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Loan Recovery Officer",
  "description": "Maywood Lending Limited is a financial organization dedicated to meeting the financial needs of citizens across Nigeria by providing accessible loan services whenever our customers require financial support.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position : Loan Recovery Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Agidingbi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Reviewing the company debtor list.
	Contacting customers and informing them of their overdue bills.
	Advising customers on their payment options and suggesting methods of payments.
	Negotiating suitable payment plans.


Qualifications


	Candidates should possess minimum of First School Leaving Certificate with relevant experiences.
	Candidate living within Ogba, Ikeja, Agege, Ojodu, Abule-Egba, Iyana-Ipaja Ketu, Ojota are encouraged to apply.


Salary
N140,000 - N200,000 / Month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;idowu@maywood.com.ng&nbsp;using the Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-21",

  },

Loan Recovery Officer Job at Maywood Lending Limited




        
    

        
        Maywood Lending Limited is a financial organization dedicated to meeting the financial needs of citizens across Nigeria by providing accessible loan services whenever our customers require financial support.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position : Loan Recovery Officer

Job Location: Agidingbi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Reviewing the company debtor list.
	Contacting customers and informing them of their overdue bills.
	Advising customers on their payment options and suggesting methods of payments.
	Negotiating suitable payment plans.


Qualifications


	Candidates should possess minimum of First School Leaving Certificate with relevant experiences.
	Candidate living within Ogba, Ikeja, Agege, Ojodu, Abule-Egba, Iyana-Ipaja Ketu, Ojota are encouraged to apply.


Salary
N140,000 - N200,000 / Month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Bank Teller",
  "description": "Catilas Resources Limited is an outsourcing and advisory services Company. Our focus is to help our clients leverage their core capabilities to optimize market opportunities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bank Teller

Job Location:&nbsp;Port Harcourt / Trans Amadi, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Catilas Resources Limited on behalf of our client, a leading commercial Bank urgently needs the services of a Bank Teller.
	The ideal candidate should possess the following responsibilities:


Cash Handling:


	Accepting cash deposits from customers.
	Withdrawing cash for customers against their account balances.
	Processing currency exchanges.


Cheque Transactions:


	Accepting cheque deposits from customers.
	Cashing cheques for account holders.
	Verifying the authenticity of cheques.


Customer Service:


	Assisting customers with their inquiries and providing information about their accounts.
	Addressing customer concerns and resolving issues related to transactions.
	Promoting products and services to customers.


Transaction Recording:


	Accurately recording all transactions in the bank&rsquo;s computer systems.
	Maintaining and updating customer account information.


End-of-Day Balancing:


	Balancing the cash drawer at the end of each day to ensure accuracy.
	Reconciling transaction records with the actual cash on hand.


Compliance:


	Adhering to banking regulations and policies.
	Ensuring compliance with anti-money laundering (AML) and know your customer (KYC) guidelines.


Cross-Selling:


	Identifying opportunities to cross-sell additional bank products or services to customers.
	Recommending products such as savings accounts, fixed deposits, or credit cards.


Security:


	Following security protocols to prevent fraud and unauthorized access.
	Reporting any suspicious activities or transactions.


Educational Support:


	Assisting customers in understanding various bank products and services.
	Guiding customers on the use of ATMs, online banking, and other self-service options.


Team Collaboration:


	Collaborating with other staff members, including loan officers, branch managers, and customer service representatives.
	Participating in training sessions and meetings to stay updated on banking procedure


Requirements


	Minimum of HND/Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in any discipline.
	Minimum of Lower Credit or Second Class Lower Division
	NYSC completion / exemption certificate
	Age limit is 29 years or below.


Salary


	200,000 naira monthly
	Other Benefits: HMO, 13th Month and Leave allowance.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to:&nbsp;fazeez@catilas.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-14",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",

  },
  "jobLocation": {

Bank Teller Job at Catilas Resources Limited




        
    

        
        Catilas Resources Limited is an outsourcing and advisory services Company. Our focus is to help our clients leverage their core capabilities to optimize market opportunities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bank Teller

Job Location: Port Harcourt / Trans Amadi, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Catilas Resources Limited on behalf of our client, a leading commercial Bank urgently needs the services of a Bank Teller.
	The ideal candidate should possess the following responsibilities:


Cash Handling:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Accepting cash deposits from customers.
	Withdrawing cash for customers against their account balances.
	Processing currency exchanges.


Cheque Transactions:


	Accepting cheque deposits from customers.
	Cashing cheques for account holders.
	Verifying the authenticity of cheques.


Customer Service:


	Assisting customers with their inquiries and providing information about their accounts.
	Addressing customer concerns and resolving issues related to transactions.
	Promoting products and services to customers.


Transaction Recording:


	Accurately recording all transactions in the bank’s computer systems.
	Maintaining and updating customer account information.


End-of-Day Balancing:


	Balancing the cash drawer at the end of each day to ensure accuracy.
	Reconciling transaction records with the actual cash on hand.


Compliance:


	Adhering to banking regulations and policies.
	Ensuring compliance with anti-money laundering (AML) and know your customer (KYC) guidelines.


Cross-Selling:


	Identifying opportunities to cross-sell additional bank products or services to customers.
	Recommending products such as savings accounts, fixed deposits, or credit cards.


Security:


	Following security protocols to prevent fraud and unauthorized access.
	Reporting any suspicious activities or transactions.


Educational Support:


	Assisting customers in understanding various bank products and services.
	Guiding customers on the use of ATMs, online banking, and other self-service options.


Team Collaboration:


	Collaborating with other staff members, including loan officers, branch managers, and customer service representatives.
	Participating in training sessions and meetings to stay updated on banking procedure


Requirements


	Minimum of HND/Bachelor’s degree in any discipline.
	Minimum of Lower Credit or Second Class Lower Division
	NYSC completion / exemption certificate
	Age limit is 29 years or below.


Salary


	200,000 naira monthly
	Other Benefits: HMO, 13th Month and Leave allowance.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Mobile Developer (React Native)",
  "description": "Vaste Holdings is building a regulated investment and savings platform for the Nigerian retail market &mdash; enabling individuals to onboard, fund a wallet, and access structured investment products through a secure, compliant digital experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mobile Developer (React Native)
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The role


	We are a growing asset management and fintech firm looking for a Cross-Platform Mobile Developer with good years of experience.
	Qualified candidate will build and maintain mobile applications for retail investors, portfolio tracking, and real-time asset management.


Key responsibilities


	Develop and maintain cross-platform mobile apps (iOS &amp; Android) using React Native.
	Build portfolio management dashboards showing asset allocation, returns, risk metrics, and transaction history.
	Integrate REST APIs for user authentication, trade execution, fund transfers, and market data.
	Implement secure storage, biometric login, certificate pinning, and encryption for fintech compliance.
	Optimize performance for large financial datasets (e.g., historical NAVs, price feeds, transaction logs).
	Collaborate in-office 2 days/week with backend engineers, product managers, and QA teams.


Required experience and skills Technical skills


	4-5 years of hands-on of professional mobile development experience.
	Strong proficiency in React Native (TypeScript).
	Proven experience in fintech, asset management, or wealth management (portfolio apps, investment dashboards, trading interfaces).
	Experience with state management (Redux, Bloc, Provider, or MobX).
	Familiarity with financial charts (MPAndroidChart, Victory Native, flchart).
	Knowledge of RESTful APIs, WebSockets (for live prices), and offline data persistence.
	Version control (Git) and CI/CD basics. &bull; Ability to work in a fast?paced but supportive fintech environment. Fintech and regulatory experience
	Working knowledge of SEC Nigeria regulations, fund management, or roboadvisory concepts.
	Experience with Nigerian fintech APIs (Flutterwave, Paystack, or banking gateways).
	Published apps on Google Play Store / Apple App Store.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Applications and referrals to:&nbsp;careers@vasteltd.com&nbsp;or&nbsp;matthew.adesina@vasteltd.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	Candidates should share their CV and a short note covering their most relevant fintech delivery experience.
	Where available, references from previous technical leadership engagements are strongly encouraged.
	Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an initial conversation with the team.

Mobile Developer (React Native) Job at Vaste Holdings




        
    

        
        Vaste Holdings is building a regulated investment and savings platform for the Nigerian retail market — enabling individuals to onboard, fund a wallet, and access structured investment products through a secure, compliant digital experience.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Mobile Developer (React Native)
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

The role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are a growing asset management and fintech firm looking for a Cross-Platform Mobile Developer with good years of experience.
	Qualified candidate will build and maintain mobile applications for retail investors, portfolio tracking, and real-time asset management.


Key responsibilities


	Develop and maintain cross-platform mobile apps (iOS & Android) using React Native.
	Build portfolio management dashboards showing asset allocation, returns, risk metrics, and transaction history.
	Integrate REST APIs for user authentication, trade execution, fund transfers, and market data.
	Implement secure storage, biometric login, certificate pinning, and encryption for fintech compliance.
	Optimize performance for large financial datasets (e.g., historical NAVs, price feeds, transaction logs).
	Collaborate in-office 2 days/week with backend engineers, product managers, and QA teams.


Required experience and skills Technical skills


	4-5 years of hands-on of professional mobile development experience.
	Strong proficiency in React Native (TypeScript).
	Proven experience in fintech, asset management, or wealth management (portfolio apps, investment dashboards, trading interfaces).
	Experience with state management (Redux, Bloc, Provider, or MobX).
	Familiarity with financial charts (MPAndroidChart, Victory Native, flchart).
	Knowledge of RESTful APIs, WebSockets (for live prices), and offline data persistence.
	Version control (Git) and CI/CD basics. • Ability to work in a fast?paced but supportive fintech environment. Fintech and regulatory experience
	Working knowledge of SEC Nigeria regulations, fund management, or roboadvisory concepts.
	Experience with Nigerian fintech APIs (Flutterwave, Paystack, or banking gateways).
	Published apps on Google Play Store / Apple App Store.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Applications and referrals to: [email protected] or [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	Candidates should share their CV and a short note covering their most relevant fintech delivery experience.
	Where available, references from previous technical leadership engagements are strongly encouraged.
	Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for an initial conversation with the team.
    

    

        Click here to Apply
